meaning: n.合法(性),正统(性),正确(性),合理(性)
examples: Sampras beat Carl-Uwe Steeb by 6-1, 6-2, 6-1 to underline the legitimacy of his challenge for the title.
Time, rather than intention, has given them legitimacy.
These ethnic- and class-based traditions themselves challenged the legitimacy of patriarchal marriage.
To haul off with these words is to leave one's moral legitimacy in question.
Otherwise, effectiveness may be achieved at the expense of popular legitimacy and, ultimately, provoke a backlash.
